title: quercus lusitanica var. alpestris
intro: Quercus lusitanica var. alpestris, also known as the Portuguese oak, is a variant of the Portuguese oak tree (Quercus lusitanica) that is native to the mountainous regions of Portugal and Spain. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus lusitanica var. alpestris include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to digestive problems.
